sourceText: Everything that is born must die. Everything that grows must decay. Everything that is high must fall. And all that is fleeting must come to an end.
Acknowledges the transient nature of life and the ever-changing state of all things, advocating for humility.
To know that we know what we know, and to know that we do not know what we do not know, that is true knowledge.
Emphasizes the importance of clearly recognizing the limits of one's knowledge and pursuing truth with humility.
Expresses the belief that love can overcome all obstacles, even in the face of adversity.
Exile from Florence due to political intrigue and confiscation of property, the death of his beloved Beatrice, and the loneliness and despair of his life in exile.

Transformed his suffering and trials into the great literary work 'Divine Comedy,' overcoming personal pain by embedding profound reflections on human existence, God, and society, leaving behind an eternal literary legacy.
Love, that moves the sun and the other stars.

In the final lines of his masterpiece, the 'Divine Comedy,' Dante sang that love is the fundamental force that moves all things in the universe. This signifies a love beyond mere human affection, a divine love, and love as a cosmic order.
The hottest places in hell are reserved for those who in times of great moral crisis maintain their neutrality.

This statement warns of the danger of remaining silent or apathetic in the face of moral dilemmas. Dante believed that even a passive stance, not actively doing good but also not condoning evil, was morally unacceptable.
You cannot find the right path without first losing the way.

This quote suggests that trials and tribulations in life's journey can be opportunities for growth. To find the right path, it is sometimes essential to experience wrong turns and learn from the process.
